questions.question_text: Before offering timeshare interests in Arkansas, a developer must:
questions.correct_answer: Register the timeshare plan with AREC
distractors: Obtain a special timeshare broker license
File with the Secretary of State only
Get approval from the local zoning board
questions.explanation: Under the Arkansas Time-Share Act (Title 18, Ch. 14), developers must register the timeshare plan with AREC before offering interests to consumers.

questions.question_text: A buyer purchases a timeshare interest in Arkansas. How many days do they have to cancel?
distractors: 3 days without penalty
10 days without penalty
7 days without penalty
questions.explanation: Under the Arkansas Time-Share Act, buyers may cancel for five calendar days after signing the contract of sale, and independently may cancel any time until they receive the public offering statement. The five-day right is mutual: the developer holds a matching right.

questions.question_text: A timeshare resale company wants to operate in Arkansas. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Register with AREC as a timeshare reseller
distractors: Obtain a standard broker's license only
Register with the Attorney General's office
No registration is required for resellers
questions.explanation: Under Act 733 of 2021, timeshare resellers and transfer agents must register with AREC before operating in Arkansas.

questions.question_text: A developer sells timeshare interests in Arkansas without registering with AREC. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: The sales are unlawful and subject to AREC enforcement
distractors: The sales are valid if buyers don't complain
Only a warning is issued for first offenses
The developer must simply register within 30 days
questions.explanation: Offering or disposing of timeshare interests without registering the plan with AREC violates the Arkansas Time-Share Act. Purchasers also remain entitled to cancel until they receive the public offering statement, and violations expose the developer to AREC enforcement.

questions.question_text: Which agency oversees both real estate licensing and timeshare regulation in Arkansas?
distractors: The Arkansas Attorney General
The Department of Commerce
The Securities Commission
questions.explanation: AREC administers both the Real Estate License Law and the Arkansas Time-Share Act, making it the single regulatory body for both areas.

questions.question_text: Who may prepare a Broker Price Opinion (BPO) in Arkansas?
distractors: Only licensed appraisers
Only principal brokers
Any licensed professional
questions.explanation: Under Arkansas Code 17-42-110, both licensed real estate brokers and salespersons may prepare BPOs. This is distinct from an appraisal, which requires an appraiser's license.

questions.question_text: A BPO in Arkansas must NOT use which of the following terms?
distractors: "Estimated price" or "probable price"
"Suggested listing price"
"Broker opinion" or "price estimate"
questions.explanation: BPOs must NOT contain the terms 'market value,' 'appraised value,' or 'appraisal.' These terms are reserved for formal appraisals by licensed appraisers.

questions.question_text: What font size and style is required for the BPO disclaimer in Arkansas?
distractors: 10-point italic type
12-point standard type
8-point fine print
questions.explanation: The BPO must include a disclaimer in 14-point bold type stating it is not an appraisal of market value. This ensures consumers clearly understand the nature of the document.

questions.question_text: A BPO prepared by a salesperson must also include the signature of:
questions.correct_answer: The principal broker or executive broker
distractors: A separately licensed appraiser
The requesting buyer's lender
An appointed AREC commissioner
questions.explanation: BPO rules require the names and signatures of the preparing licensee and the principal broker or designated executive broker, plus the firm name and date of issuance. The responsible broker answers for BPOs prepared by affiliated licensees.

questions.question_text: A licensee labels their property pricing report as a 'market value appraisal.' This:
distractors: Is acceptable if the licensee is also an appraiser
Is fine as long as it includes a disclaimer
Only matters for federally related transactions
questions.explanation: Using the term 'appraisal' or 'market value' in a BPO violates Arkansas Code 17-42-110. BPOs must clearly distinguish themselves from formal appraisals.

questions.question_text: Can AREC require licensees to join the National Association of Realtors?
questions.correct_answer: No, trade organization membership is voluntary
distractors: Yes, as a condition of licensure
Yes, but only for principal brokers
Only if the licensee joins a local board
questions.explanation: AREC cannot require licensees to be members of any trade organization. Membership in NAR, local boards, or any professional association is entirely voluntary.

questions.question_text: A broker tells a new salesperson they must join the local board of Realtors to keep their license. This statement is:
questions.correct_answer: Incorrect; no membership can be required
distractors: Correct; local board membership is mandatory
Correct for the first year of licensure
Correct if the firm is a Realtor firm
questions.explanation: AREC cannot require membership in any trade organization. While a firm may have its own policy about membership, it is not a licensing requirement.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum civil penalty for engaging in real estate activity in Arkansas without a license?
distractors: $1,000 per violation
$250 per violation
$25,000 per violation
questions.explanation: Unlicensed real estate activity carries a civil penalty of up to $5,000 per violation. (Sanctions against licensees are capped at $1,000 per violation.) Violating the license law can also be prosecuted criminally as a Class D felony.

questions.question_text: The Recovery Fund pays a consumer's claim because a licensee failed to pay AREC-ordered damages. What happens to the licensee's license?
questions.correct_answer: It is suspended until the fund is repaid with interest
distractors: Nothing, the fund payment settles the matter
It is automatically revoked permanently
It converts to inactive status for one year
questions.explanation: A licensee whose conduct causes a Recovery Fund payment has their license suspended immediately, and it may not be reinstated until the fund has been reimbursed in full, plus interest.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is considered a violation of Arkansas real estate law?
questions.correct_answer: Practicing real estate without a valid license
distractors: Declining to join a trade organization
Choosing not to use the voluntary disclosure form
Working as a part-time real estate agent
questions.explanation: Unlicensed activity is a violation. Declining trade organization membership and voluntary disclosure are specifically allowed. Part-time practice is permitted with proper licensing.
